title: xylopia ferruginea var. oxyantha
intro: Xylopia ferruginea var. oxyantha is a plant species commonly known as the yellow cardamom or Guinea pepper, belonging to the family Annonaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The plant's leaves, seeds, and bark are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, and are also used to repel insects and snakes. Xylopia ferruginea var. oxyantha can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and boosting the immune system.
